macro_segments.title: Implicit and Explicit Nazirut: Counting Days and Bringing Korbanos

micro_segments.title: Stam Nazir shaves on day 31; explicit 30-day nazir shaves on day 31 but not on day 30
Tosafot's resolution: the Tanna Kamma and Rav Matna debate concerns rabbinic status only; Torah-level, 30 days suffices
Miktzat hayom kekulo principle: can one partial day count as a full day at both beginning and end of period

macro_segments.title: Two Consecutive Naziriyos: Splitting the Final Day

micro_segments.title: Le'chatchila: first nazirut ends day 31, second begins day 31; bedieved: both end day 30 and day 60 respectively
Rubo kekulo and miktzat hayom kekulo: can one day count for two periods simultaneously

macro_segments.title: Forty Minus One: Structural Analysis of Complete Numbers in Halakha

micro_segments.title: Arba'im chasar achat in context of melachot on Shabbat and makkot; forty as a complete unit
Mikvah, Moshe on Har Sinai, 40 years in desert: 40 as transitional period
Tannaim and Amoraim dispute over exact count of melachot on Shabbat

macro_segments.title: Two Consecutive Naziriyos: Splitting the Final Day (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Papyas's testimony: explicit teaching on two consecutive naziriyos and the 30th and 60th day counts

macro_segments.title: Nazir Who Becomes Tamei: When Does Tumah Cancel Nazirut

micro_segments.title: Stam nazir becomes tamei on day 30: Tanna Kamma says soter et hakol; Rabbi Eliezer says only shiva
Explicit 30-day nazir becomes tamei on day 30: both Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Eliezer agree it cancels
100-day nazir becomes tamei on day 100: Tanna Kamma cancels all; Rabbi Eliezer cancels only 30 days
100-day nazir becomes tamei on day 101: both agree only 30 days cancel (hair growth requirement)
Rabbi Eliezer's principle: anything after yom milat (completion day) only cancels seven days of tahara, not the nezirut itself

macro_segments.title: Reconciling Rabbi Eliezer's Position Across Different Scenarios

micro_segments.title: Gemara's question: why does explicit 30-day nazir behave differently from stam nazir if Rabbi Eliezer has consistent principle
Resolution: explicit 30-day must mean shelemim (complete days); this changes the halakha even for Rabbi Eliezer
Connection to Bar Pada and Rav Matna machlokes on Daf Hei: how to count miktzat hayom kekulo

macro_segments.title: Nazir in the Cemetery: Does Nazirut Take Effect While Tamei

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: person in cemetery takes nazir vow; 30 days there counts for nothing; does not bring korban tumah
Yatzah venichnas: if he left and re-entered cemetery, days count and korban tumah applies
Rabbi Eliezer's caveat: not on the seventh day (day of tahara completion); must have rishonnim (plural days) to count

macro_segments.title: Gemara Debate: Rabbi Yochanan vs Reish Lakish on Whether Nazirut Takes Effect While Tamei

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: nazirut takes effect as matla talya (hanging thing); suspends and waits to take hold once tahara achieved
Reish Lakish: nazirut does not take effect; only becomes effective if person re-accepts it after becoming tahor
First challenge from Rabbi Yochanan: Mishnah says ein korban tumah (no tumah offering), implying he is a nazir with exemptions
Reish Lakish's defense: he is not a nazir at all; ein batoras tumah (not in the category of tumah)
Second challenge: Tosefta says one who was tamei and became nazir is forbidden wine, shaving, and tamei lemeitim; gets malkot
Reish Lakish's defense: case must involve yatzah venichnas (left and re-entered); person affirmed nazirut after becoming tahor
Third challenge from Baraita: tamei who became nazir counts 7th day of tahara toward 30; nazir tahor who became tamei does not count 7th day

macro_segments.title: Rambam's Position on Nazir in Cemetery and Malkot

micro_segments.title: Rambam: nazir tahor who intentionally became tamei gets malkot; violates lo yitma and bal tacher
Rambam: person in cemetery who became nazir still violates bal yachel devaro and bal tacher
Lechem Mishnah's discussion: lav she'ein bo maise (prohibition without action); how standing in cemetery qualifies as maise for malkot

macro_segments.title: Counting Days vs Prohibitions: Distinguishing Two Aspects of Nazirut

micro_segments.title: Key distinction: person in cemetery is a nazir (prohibitions apply immediately) but 30-day count only begins after tahara
Prohibitions (wine, shaving, tumah) function like regular neder and extend throughout; counting requires consecutive tahor days
Application: person in cemetery 20 days, leaves, purifies 7 days; day 27 begins the 30-day count, ending day 56

topical_tags.term: Bar Pada vs Rav Matna machlokes
Bal yachel devaro (do not profane your word)
